codeReview.summary: Pass. This rerun resolves the original blocker: `src/content/vault-capacity.ts:473-549` now routes `computeVaultCapacityEstimate` through `computeBiddingAllocations`, so the calculator inherits the shipped âˆš-weighted bidding model, $100 floor, and per-staker cap instead of the old linear share formula, and the user-facing vault copy in `src/components/tokenomics/FirstVaultPolicy.tsx`, `src/components/tokenomics/VaultCapacityModel.tsx`, and `src/content/token-tiers.ts` no longer renders visible `per-wallet` wording. A smaller modeling caveat remains in the representative-pool synthesis, but it does not reintroduce the prior scrutiny failure.

issues.description: `computeVaultCapacityEstimate` models all remaining stake as 100k-BETTER peers and then silently truncates the synthetic pool to `maxStakers` at lines 510-522. For first-vault scenarios, any `totalStaked` above roughly `userStake + 24.9M BETTER` stops affecting the modeled allocation because excess stake is discarded before `computeBiddingAllocations` runs. In local reproduction, a 200k stake with a $25k vault returns the same `$120` effective estimate at 50M, 100M, and 200M total staked. The rerun still fixes the original blocker, but the adjustable `totalStaked` input now has an undisclosed saturation point that could surprise users.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance about the corrected vault cap is still internally inconsistent: several shared-state artifacts continue to preserve `per-wallet` terminology even though VAL-TOKEN-017 says no such reference should remain anywhere. This fix corrected the shipped UI, but future workers can still copy the stale phrasing from mission docs and library notes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/AGENTS.md:43` still says '(not per-wallet)'; `/Users/test/.factory/missions/33dae310-26cf-4368-9488-64b235b6db83/validation-contract.md:191-199` still describes a 'first-vault per-wallet deposit cap' before forbidding any per-wallet reference; `/Users/test/vision/.factory/skills/content-strategy-worker/SKILL.md:27-29` still says '$25k TOTAL vault cap (not per-wallet)'; `/Users/test/vision/.factory/library/use-case-analysis.md:128` still says '$25K per-wallet deposit cap'.

summary: Pass. The fix removes the user-visible `per-wallet` wording and moves the interactive vault calculator onto the shipped âˆš-weighted bidding model with the $100 floor and per-staker cap, so it clears the original scrutiny failure. I noted one non-blocking estimation-fidelity caveat in the synthetic staker-pool logic plus a shared-state documentation drift around lingering `per-wallet` terminology.
